item.title: Integration tests for cloud provider APIs — replace fetch mocks with contract tests
item.intent: Cloud provider API integrations (DigitalOcean, Hetzner, GCP, AWS) are tested entirely against mocked fetch responses. If a provider changes their API response format, ClawHQ will break silently. Contract tests must verify our code handles real API shapes.
item.description: 10 test files mock globalThis.fetch to simulate cloud provider API responses. The mocks return hardcoded JSON that may not match the real API. Four providers are implemented: DigitalOcean (src/cloud/provisioning/providers/digitalocean.ts), Hetzner (hetzner.ts), GCP (gcp.ts), AWS (aws.ts). Each has a test file with fetch mocked. No test ever hits a real API or validates against a real response schema. This item adds contract tests: (1) record real API responses (sanitized) as fixtures, (2) validate our parsing logic against those fixtures, (3) optionally run live API tests when credentials are available (CI secret). This does NOT remove unit test mocks — it adds a second layer of validation.
steps: Record real API responses from each provider (sanitized — no real IPs, keys, or account IDs)
Write contract tests that parse real API response shapes through our provider code
Add optional live API test mode: if CLAWHQ_TEST_DO_TOKEN is set, run against real DigitalOcean API (create+destroy a $4 droplet)
Document how to run live tests safely in CONTRIBUTING.md
acceptanceCriteria: Contract tests validate provider code against real API response shapes (recorded fixtures)
Tests catch response format changes that would break parsing (field renames, type changes, new required fields)
Live API tests run when provider credentials are available, skip gracefully when not
Existing unit tests with mocks continue to pass unchanged
